violations.description: All three E2E ATDD journeys are skipped, reducing acceptance-level confidence for end-to-end ingress behavior.
violations.suggestion: Re-enable skipped E2E ATDD journeys and gate with deterministic signing fixture compatibility.
violations.code_snippet: test.skip('[P0] end-to-end ingress journey accepts valid signed webhook ...')

violations.description: Tests use priority tags but do not include explicit story-level test IDs required by the test-level framework convention.
violations.suggestion: Add explicit IDs (e.g., e.1-API-001) to improve traceability from AC to test case.
violations.code_snippet: test('[P0] invalid webhook signatures are refused fail-closed ... @P0', async (...) => {

violations.description: E2E suite validates workflow mostly through API calls; limited browser-driven interaction assertions reduce UI contract confidence.
violations.suggestion: Add at least one browser-centric assertion path for operator-visible outcomes where applicable.

recommendations: Unskip E2E ATDD scenarios for AC-level end-to-end confidence
Annotate tests with explicit traceable IDs in addition to priority markers
Expand one or more E2E checks to assert operator-visible UI effects directly
summary: Coverage is acceptable but constrained by skipped E2E ATDD scenarios and traceability-ID gaps.
